title: Sorption and separation studies of thorium(IV) in L-valine medium using  poly[dibenzo-18-crown-6]
author: Sandip Sabale
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Analytical and Environmental Research Lab, Department of Chemistry, Shivaji University, Kolhapur-416 004, 

 Maharashtra, India 

  E-mail :  bsmohite@hotmail.com 

  Manuscript received 4 March 2008, revised 22 May 2009, accepted 20 August 2009  

 A simple method has been developed for the separation of thorium(IV) In L-valine medium using poly[dlbenzo-18-crown-61 and column chromatography. The sorption of thorium(IV) was quantitative from 1x 10 -4  to 1 X 10 -1   M  L-valine. Amongst the various eluents 0.5-8.0  M  hydrobromic acid, perchloric acid, hydrochloric acid and 0.5-5.0 M sulfuric acid were round to be efficient eluents for thorium(IV), The capacity of poly[dlbenzo-18-crown-61 for thorium(IV) was 0.258 mmol/g of crown polymer. Thorium(IV) was separated from number or cations and anions in binary mixtures. Most or the cations and anions showed very high tolerance limit. Selective separation or thorium(IV) was possible from uranium(VI), cerium(lll) and lanthanum(lll), The method was extended to determine thorium(IV) from geological and Industrial samples.
